title: Prison Cells After N Days
hints: Notice the state space is tiny. Since there are only 256 possible configurations, the sequence of states must eventually repeat.
Simulate day by day while storing each seen state and its day index. Stop as soon as a state repeats to find the cycle length.
Use the cycle length to fast-forward N days. The final answer is the state at index (N minus start of cycle) modulo cycle length, plus the start offset.
